classObjective: Graph the solution set of a one-step inequality on a number line and write an inequality that matches a given number-line graph.
workedExamples: 6-1, Example 1 — Graph Solution Sets of Inequalities on a Number Line
warmUp: Graph x > 3 and x <= 5 on a number line
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: open vs. closed circles; shading direction; 'at least' and 'at most' language interpretation
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice graphing one-step inequalities and writing inequalities from number-line graphs
independentPractice: Practice problems graphing inequality solution sets and matching graphs to inequalities
assessment: Exit ticket: write the inequality shown on a number line and graph the inequality x < -2
capReflection: How does knowing the direction of the inequality symbol help you determine which way to shade on a number line?
